review_comment: I've changed the condition to (r < 0) but kept "return r" because we changed the return value of posix_fallocate_loop() to be negative on errors.
ha, the old check was entirely borked even...
Now it should work after return value negation on errors.

I'd prefer
```C
RateLimit rl = { 10 * USEC_PER_SEC, 16 };
```
but OK.
Well, I don't see other (at least many) examples of this "C++-style" variables declaration in the middle of a function.
Actually, I couldn't have find any example (by a brief looking across different files).
But I can move it to the start of the function.

There is no need to move the definition up. Just define it in the exact same place where it was initialized before (and where I made the comment…).

> Well, I don't see other (at least many) examples of this "C++-style" variables declaration in the middle of a function.

The style changed. Old code pushed all variable declarations to the top of the function, new code doesn't do that, at least not always.

But anyway, it's a minor style issue, we can merge this as is.
